text: Sons born of wives of the same *varṇa* are of the same *varṇa*. // Vi_16.1 //
Those born of wives in the direct order of castes are of the same *varṇa* as their mother. // Vi_16.2 //
Those born of wives in the reverse order of castes are despised by the Āryas. // Vi_16.3 //
Therein, the son of a Vaiśyā by a Śūdra is an *āyogava*. // Vi_16.4 //
The *pulkasa* and the *māgadha* are the sons of a Kṣatriyā by a Vaiśya and a Śūdra respectively. // Vi_16.5 //
And the *caṇḍāla*, the *vaidehaka*, and the *sūta* are the sons of a Brāhmaṇī by a Śūdra, a Vaiśya, and a Kṣatriya respectively. // Vi_16.6 //
And the mixed castes from these unions are innumerable. // Vi_16.7 //
Stage-performance is the occupation of the *āyogavas*. // Vi_16.8 //
Hunting is the occupation of the *pulkasas*. // Vi_16.9 //
The act of praise is the occupation of the *māgadhas*. // Vi_16.10 //
Execution of criminals is the occupation of the *caṇḍālas*. // Vi_16.11 //
Guarding women and living by that is the occupation of the *vaidehakas*. // Vi_16.12 //
Horse-charioteering is the occupation of the *sūtas*. // Vi_16.13 //
For *caṇḍālas*, residence outside the village and wearing the clothes of the dead are special rules. // Vi_16.14 //
And for all of them, marriage is with those of the same caste. // Vi_16.15 //
And they inherit their own father's property. // Vi_16.16 //

These castes born of mixed unions, as shown by their fathers and mothers,
 Whether hidden or manifest, are to be known by their occupations. || Vi_16.17 ||

Giving up one's life for the sake of a Brāhmaṇa or a cow, without expectation of reward,
 And in defense of women and children, is a cause of salvation for the outcastes. || Vi_16.18 ||
